title: Diagnostic Medical Sonographers
description: Produce ultrasonic recordings of internal organs for use by physicians. Includes vascular technologists.

tasks.text: Observe screen during scan to ensure that image produced is satisfactory for diagnostic purposes, making adjustments to equipment as required.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Observe and care for patients throughout examinations to ensure their safety and comfort.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide sonogram and oral or written summary of technical findings to physician for use in medical diagnosis.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select appropriate equipment settings and adjust patient positions to obtain the best sites and angles.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Operate ultrasound equipment to produce and record images of the motion, shape, and composition of blood, organs, tissues, or bodily masses, such as fluid accumulations.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Decide which images to include, looking for differences between healthy and pathological areas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare patient for exam by explaining procedure, transferring patient to ultrasound table, scrubbing skin and applying gel, and positioning patient properly.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Determine whether scope of exam should be extended, based on findings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Obtain and record accurate patient history, including prior test results or information from physical examinations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain records that include patient information, sonographs and interpretations, files of correspondence, publications and regulations, or quality assurance records, such as pathology, biopsy, or post-operative reports.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Record and store suitable images, using camera unit connected to the ultrasound equipment.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Coordinate work with physicians or other healthcare team members, including providing assistance during invasive procedures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Clean, check, and maintain sonographic equipment, submitting maintenance requests or performing minor repairs as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform clerical duties, such as scheduling exams or special procedures, keeping records, or archiving computerized images.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform legal and ethical duties, including preparing safety or accident reports, obtaining written consent from patient to perform invasive procedures, or reporting symptoms of abuse or neglect.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise or train students or other medical sonographers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform medical procedures, such as administering oxygen, inserting and removing airways, taking vital signs, or giving emergency treatment, such as first aid or cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR).
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Maintain stock and supplies, preparing supplies for special examinations and ordering supplies when necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Process and code film from procedures and complete appropriate documentation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Load and unload film cassettes used to record images from procedures.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.
